question: Is the sailing ship model (HMS Endeavour) clearly present in the image?
Is the ship placed specifically in the empty gap in the middle of the third tier of the bookshelf?
Does the ship retain key visual features of the source image (three masts, cream sails, dark wooden hull, and rigging)?
Is the black display stand of the ship model visible beneath the hull?
Is the ship scaled to fit strictly within the gap, avoiding overlap with the shelf above or the books to the sides?
Is the original white background completely removed from around the ship, leaving no visible halos or matte lines?
Are the intricate rigging lines and mast details of the ship preserved without blurring or jagged artifacts?
Does the ship appear to rest flat on the shelf surface with correct perspective, rather than floating or tilting unnaturally?
Is the lighting on the ship adapted to match the warm, dimmer ambient tone of the bookshelf environment?
Does the ship cast a realistic shadow on the shelf surface consistent with the overhead recessed lighting?
Are the books immediately to the left and right of the inserted ship preserved without distortion?
Is the globe on the second tier of the bookshelf preserved and unchanged?
Is the clock on the fourth tier of the bookshelf preserved and unchanged?
Is the wooden structure of the bookshelf preserved without warping or distortion around the insertion area?
Is the image free from visible noise, pixelation, or unnatural blurring in the edited region?
